content.text:
Digital Transformation
Semester:
2  |
Type:
UEF22  |
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30 / TD: 1h30  |
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
Understand the challenges and opportunities of digital transformation
Assimilate the key concepts and technologies of DMS and virtualisation (Cloud, big data, AI, etc.)
Design and implement digital transformation solutions
Develop skills in digital project management and change management in a digital context
Analyse the impact of digital transformation on organisations
Recommended prior knowledge:
Information systems, databases.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Digital Transformation and Information Systems
Definition and challenges of digital transformation
Role of IS in digital transformation
Different phases and types of digital transformation (technological, organisational, cultural)
Key success factors; impacts on organisations and jobs
Change management; role of the CIO in a digital transformation context
Emerging Technologies and Digital Transformation
Big Data: collection, storage, processing and analysis of massive data
Cloud Computing: architectures, services (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS), security challenges
Artificial intelligence (AI): machine learning, deep learning, enterprise applications
Internet of Things (IoT): sensors, networks, impact on business processes and services
Data security and cybersecurity: threats, data protection, compliance (GDPR, etc.)
Collaborative tools and digital platforms
DMS and Virtualisation in Digital Transformation
Role of DMS: document dematerialisation, process automation, improved collaboration, cost reduction
Role of virtualisation: flexibility and agility, cost reduction, efficiency, rapid deployment
Document Management Systems (DMS)
DMS concepts and challenges (definition, objectives, advantages, types of digital documents)
Key stages of a DMS project (collection, indexing, storage, distribution, archiving)
DMS project implementation (needs analysis, tool selection, planning, development and deployment)
DMS Tools and Technologies
Content management systems (CMS)
Document capture and scanning software
Records management information systems
Collaboration and document sharing tools
Electronic archiving solutions
Analysis of CRM, ERP and other IS linked to DMS
Emerging technologies in DMS (AI, blockchain, etc.)
Legal and Regulatory Aspects of DMS
Legislation on the conservation of electronic documents
Sector-specific regulations (health, finance, etc.)
Evidentiary value of electronic documents
Personal data protection and GDPR legal framework
IS Virtualisation
Concepts and challenges: definition, types (server, network, storage, desktop)
Implementation: platform selection, virtual machine management, backup and restore
Tools and technologies: VMware, Hyper-V, etc.; monitoring and performance management
Assessment
In-person semester exam (60%)
Continuous assessment (CC) (40%): continuous tests, mini-project
TP:
Creation and management of virtual machines.
TD:
Analysis of case studies of companies that have virtualised their IS.
References
Veldhoven Van Ziboud and Vanthienen Jan (2019). Designing a Comprehensive Understanding of Digital Transformation and its Impact.
Peter C. Verhoef et al. (2021). Digital transformation: A multidisciplinary reflection and research agenda.
Journal of Business Research
, Volume 122.
David Greenspan (2010).
Virtualization: A Practical Guide
.
Scott Lowe (2014).
Mastering VMware vSphere
.
